body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Intention-Based Development adoption
body.description:
**What changed:** Adopted Intention-Based Development — intention: field on tickets, IBD preamble in DickSimnel ToolLoop, TOO annotated with I-intend-that headers.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- intention: field adds a required step that could slow /sorted if integration is awkward or the field prompt is unclear
- DickSimnel IBD preamble could cause turn-1 to be consumed by intention-statement boilerplate instead of productive work (longer time-to-first-tool-call)
- TOO annotation pass could drift stale faster than architecture notes if intention statements are vague (unfalsifiable intentions rot)
- 'Losing fewer points' signal is subjective — if no fake-DONEs occur anyway, the signal is ambiguous

**Signals to watch:**
- /sorted usage: does intention: field get populated with real content or boilerplate 'I intend that this ticket is done'?
- DickSimnel turn logs: does turn 1 show genuine hypothesis statements before tool calls begin?
- Akien friction: does IBD language feel natural or forced in conversation?
- Fake-DONE rate (T-fake-done count): lower after IBD preamble ships?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-18 or when 5 DickSimnel tickets have shipped post-preamble, whichever comes first.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed
